name: static

metadata.name: static
metadata.summary: Serve static or templated content via WSGI or stand-alone.

documentation:
# static

Serve static or templated content via WSGI or stand-alone.

Install Static

    $ pip install static

Serve up some content:

    $ static localhost 9999 static-content/

Or in the context of a WSGI application:

```python
import static
wsgi_app = static.Cling('/var/www')
```

You can also use Python template strings, Moustache templates or 
easily roll your own templating plugin. See the tests and source
code for examples.

Pull requests welcome. Happy hacking.

modules.name: static.apps
documentation:
static - A very simple WSGI way to serve static (or mixed) content.

(See the docstrings of the various functions and classes.)
!/usr/bin/env python2.4

functions.name: static.apps._iter_and_close
documentation: Yield file contents by block then close the file.

functions.name: static.apps.cling_wrap
documentation:
Return a Cling that serves from the given package and dir_name.

This uses pkg_resources.resource_filename which is not the
recommended way, since it extracts the files.

I think this works fine unless you have some more serious
requirements for static content, in which case you probably
shouldn't be serving it through a WSGI app.

classes.name: static.apps.StatusApp
documentation: A WSGI app that just returns the given status.

classes.name: static.apps.Cling

methods.name: static.apps.Cling.__call__
documentation: Respond to a request when called in the usual WSGI way.

methods.name: static.apps.Cling._full_path
documentation: Return the full path from which to read.

methods.name: static.apps.Cling._is_under_root
documentation: Guard against arbitrary file retrieval.

methods.name: static.apps.Cling._guess_type
documentation: Guess the mime type using the mimetypes module.

methods.name: static.apps.Cling._conditions
documentation: Return a tuple of etag, last_modified by mtime from stat.

methods.name: static.apps.Cling._file_like
documentation: Return the appropriate file object.

methods.name: static.apps.Cling._body
documentation: Return an iterator over the body of the response.

A very simple way to serve static content via WSGI.

Serve the file of the same path as PATH_INFO in self.datadir.

Look up the Content-type in self.content_types by extension
or use 'text/plain' if the extension is not found.

Serve up the contents of the file or delegate to self.not_found.

classes.name: static.apps.Shock

methods.name: static.apps.Shock._match_magic
documentation: Return the first magic that matches this path or None.

methods.name: static.apps.Shock._full_path
documentation: Return the full path from which to read.

methods.name: static.apps.Shock._guess_type
documentation: Guess the mime type magically or using the mimetypes module.

methods.name: static.apps.Shock._conditions
documentation: Return Etag and Last-Modified values defaults to now for both.

methods.name: static.apps.Shock._file_like
documentation: Return the appropriate file object.

methods.name: static.apps.Shock._body
documentation: Return an iterator over the body of the response.

A very simple way to serve up mixed content.

Serves static content just like Cling (it's superclass)
except that it process content with the first matching
"magic" from self.magics if any apply.

See Cling and classes with "Magic" in their names in this module.

If you are using Shock with the StringMagic class for instance:

shock = Shock('/data', magics=[StringMagic(food='cheese')])

Let's say you have a file called /data/foo.txt.stp containing one line:

"I love to eat $food!"

When you do a GET on /foo.txt you will see this in your browser:

"I love to eat cheese!"

This is really nice if you have a color variable in your css files or
something trivial like that. It seems silly to create or change a
handful of objects for a couple of dynamic bits of text.

classes.name: static.apps.BaseMagic

methods.name: static.apps.BaseMagic.exists
documentation: Check that self.new_path(full_path) exists.

methods.name: static.apps.BaseMagic.new_path
documentation: Add the self.extension to the path.

methods.name: static.apps.BaseMagic.old_path
documentation: Remove self.extension.

methods.name: static.apps.BaseMagic.matches
documentation: Check that path ends with self.extension.

methods.name: static.apps.BaseMagic.conditions
documentation: Return Etag and Last-Modified values (based on mtime).

methods.name: static.apps.BaseMagic.file_like
documentation: Return a file object for path.

methods.name: static.apps.BaseMagic.body
documentation: Return an iterator over the body of the response.

Base class for magic file handling.

Really a do nothing if you were to use this directly.

In a strait forward case you would just override .extension and body().
(See StringMagic in this module for a simple example of subclassing.)

In a more complex case you may need to override many or all methods.

classes.name: static.apps.StringMagic

methods.name: static.apps.StringMagic.__init__
documentation: Keyword arguments populate self.variables.

methods.name: static.apps.StringMagic.body
documentation:
Pass environ and self.variables in to template.

self.variables overrides environ so that suprises in environ don't
cause unexpected output if you are passing a value in explicitly.

Magic to replace variables in file contents using string.Template.

Using this requires Python2.4.

classes.name: static.apps.MoustacheMagic

methods.name: static.apps.MoustacheMagic.body
documentation: Pass environ and **self.variables into the template.
Like StringMagic only using Moustache templates.
